rows: All 300 branch warehouses are connected via legacy Multiprotocol Label Switching (MPLS) circuits procured from a single incumbent carrier. Bandwidth tiers are rigid (2 Mbps to 10 Mbps per branch), costly to upgrade, and require 60–90-day lead times for any configuration change.

The Company experienced an average of 4.3 unplanned network outages per month per region in FY2024–25, each lasting an average of 47 minutes. These outages directly halt warehouse management system (WMS) operations, fleet tracking updates, and ERP transaction processing, resulting in an estimated revenue impact of INR 3.8 Crore per month.

Hub warehouse sites (Tier-1) currently operate on a single MPLS link with no active failover path. Loss of connectivity at a hub site cascades to 8–15 dependent spoke sites reliant on the hub for WMS access and IoT aggregation.

Apex is executing a cloud-first transformation programme (ERP on Microsoft Azure, data analytics on AWS). Currently, all cloud traffic is backhauled through the Mumbai Data Centre, introducing latency of 180–340 ms for branch-to-cloud workloads and consuming disproportionate Data Centre bandwidth.

Branch sites lack next-generation firewall (NGFW) protection. A significant ransomware incident in Q3 FY2024 resulted in operational downtime of 72 hours across 14 warehouse sites and a forensic investigation cost of INR 1.1 Crore. Apex currently has no 24/7 Security Operations Centre (SOC) capability.

Apex operates a fleet of 2.5 million IoT-enabled tracking SIMs across its vehicle fleet and warehouse assets. SIM Management is currently distributed across three different Mobile Virtual Network Operators (MVNOs), resulting in fragmented visibility, inconsistent data plans, and inability to enforce unified MDM policies.

vlm_extraction.caption: 5.2 Requirements Matrix
rows: The SD-WAN solution MUST be a cloud-managed, fully software-defined overlay capable of supporting all 300 Apex branch sites within a single management domain without requiring a hardware controller at the Data Centre.
Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP) MUST be supported for all CPE devices. A branch site MUST be fully operational within 4 hours of CPE delivery, without requiring an on-site engineer from the vendor.
Dynamic path failover from primary to secondary link MUST complete in under 50 milliseconds as measured end-to-end from packet loss detection to traffic rerouting, without manual intervention.

The solution MUST support BGP routing with automated route advertisement and withdrawal upon link failure, supporting integration with Apex's Mumbai DC edge router (Cisco ASR 1002-HX) via eBGP peering.

Vendor MUST specify the OEM hardware partner(s) for SD-WAN CPE (e.g., Cisco, Fortinet, VMware Velocloud, Palo Alto). CPE must be provided as-a-Service under OpEx pricing. Vendor-owned hardware is mandatory; Apex will not purchase CPE outright.

The solution MUST support a minimum of 8 QoS traffic classes with guaranteed bandwidth (GBW) and maximum bandwidth (MBW) policies configurable per application, per site. Guaranteed bandwidth for SAP S/4HANA and WMS traffic must be configurable independently of other traffic classes.

Integrated NGFW capability at the SD-WAN edge MUST include IPS/IDS, URL filtering (minimum 60 URL categories), DNS security, and SSL deep inspection. Vendor must specify the OEM firewall solution (e.g., Fortinet FortiGate, Palo Alto Networks NGFW, Check Point).

A self-service portal with role-based access control (RBAC) MUST be provided to Apex IT staff with read-only visibility of network topology, link health, traffic statistics, and active alerts across all 300 sites. No additional licensing cost for portal access.

Vendor MUST provide a detailed 300-site migration project plan conforming to either PRINCE2 or PMBOK methodology. A dedicated Project Manager (minimum PMP or PRINCE2 Practitioner certified) must be named in the proposal and committed to the programme through completion.

Vendor MUST hold an active Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute Partner designation and provide two (2) x 10 Gbps circuits in active-active configuration to the Azure Central India region. Evidence of Microsoft partner designation MUST be attached as Appendix B.

Vendor MUST hold an active AWS Direct Connect Delivery Partner designation and provide one (1) x 10 Gbps Dedicated Connection to the AWS ap-south-1 Mumbai region. Evidence of AWS partner designation MUST be attached as Appendix B.

The Azure ExpressRoute interconnect MUST achieve a maximum round-trip latency of 8 ms between the Apex Mumbai DC and the Azure Central India region under steady-state traffic conditions. Vendor must provide benchmark test evidence from equivalent deployments.

The Managed SOC service MUST operate 24 hours per day, 7 days per week, 365 days per year (including Indian public holidays) with no scheduled maintenance windows that reduce monitoring coverage below Tier-1 analyst staffing levels.

The SIEM platform used by the SOC MUST ingest logs from all 300 SD-WAN edge devices, the Mumbai DC, and cloud interconnects. The vendor must name the SIEM product proposed (e.g., Microsoft Sentinel, Splunk Enterprise Security, IBM QRadar, or equivalent). SIEM data MUST be stored in India.

P1 incidents MUST be acknowledged within 15 minutes and contained within 4 hours. P2 incidents MUST be acknowledged within 30 minutes and resolved within 8 hours. P3 incidents within 2 hours acknowledgement and 24 hours resolution. All timings measured 24x7.

The SOC MUST operate Incident Management, Problem Management, and Change Management processes conforming to ITIL v4. Root Cause Analysis (RCA) reports for P1 and P2 incidents MUST be delivered within 72 hours of incident containment. Vendor must provide sample RCA template with proposal.

Vendor MUST demonstrate active subscriptions to a minimum of two (2) commercial Threat Intelligence platforms and provide evidence of integration with the proposed SIEM, including IOC ingestion frequency and automated blocking workflow.

Vendor MUST provide a written Data Privacy Impact Assessment (DPIA) framework for the proposed solution, demonstrating how personal data of Apex employees and customers processed across the network is handled in compliance with the Digital Personal Data Protection

The Enterprise Mobility platform MUST support a dedicated private APN for Apex IoT SIM traffic with routing directly into Apex's SD-WAN fabric via private peering. Traffic from IoT SIMs MUST NOT transit the public internet.

The EMM platform MUST support GSMA-compliant eSIM / eUICC remote provisioning for all 2.5 million SIMs at Apex's option. Vendor must provide the GSMA eSIM RSP (Remote SIM Provisioning) compliance certificate with the proposal.

The vendor MUST provide demonstrable 4G LTE network coverage (own network or roaming agreement) covering a minimum of 98% of Apex's Tier-1 and Tier-2 site locations across all 28 operational states. A geo-referenced coverage report must be submitted with the proposal.

The EMM platform MUST expose a documented RESTful API (OpenAPI 3.0 specification) for SIM lifecycle management (activation, deactivation, suspension, data plan change) and consumption reporting. API documentation must be provided as Appendix F.

The vendor's managed service platform (SD-WAN controller, SOC SIEM, EMM platform) must operate from geo-redundant infrastructure within India with an RPO of less than 15 minutes and an RTO of less than 60 minutes for all Tier-1 managed services.

The vendor's NOC and SOC ticketing systems MUST integrate bi-directionally with Apex's ServiceNow ITSM instance for automated incident creation, status updates, and closure. Vendor must describe the integration mechanism (native connector / REST API / email) and provide a sample integration architecture.

Vendor MUST provide a unified executive dashboard and monthly managed service report covering network availability, cloud interconnect performance, SOC incident statistics, IoT SIM health, and SLA performance against all KPIs defined in Section 3.2.

Single consolidated PDF/A (ISO 19005 compliant) containing the complete proposal, including all appendices and supporting documents.
English only. All technical specifications, SLA commitments, and commercial terms must be in English.
Main body (Sections 1–12): Maximum 120 pages (A4, minimum 11pt Arial, 25mm margins). Appendices are not subject to a page limit.
Proposals must follow the identical section numbering as this RFP. Reordering of sections is not permitted.
The requirements matrix (Section 5.2) must be reproduced in its entirety with Vendor Response and Vendor Remarks columns completed. Requirement IDs must not be altered.
Pricing must be included within the same consolidated document in a clearly marked section titled "COMMERCIAL PROPOSAL — STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL", placed at the end of the document.

Any deviations from this RFP's requirements or proposed contractual terms must be compiled in a single 'Deviations Register' table as Appendix G. Deviations embedded in the main proposal text without corresponding Appendix G entry will be disregarded.
Vendor Financials — Audited annual financial statements for FY2022–23, FY2023–24, and FY2024–25

Audited financial statements FY22–23, FY23–24, FY24–25
Vendor and all subcontractors with >20% of delivery scope must hold a current, in-scope ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certification from an accredited body.
Vendor must hold ISO/IEC 20000-1:2018 (IT Service Management) certification demonstrating ITIL-aligned service delivery capability.
Vendor must provide a SOC 2 Type II report (not older than 12 months) covering Security, Availability, and Confidentiality Trust Service Criteria for all cloud-managed service components.

Vendor must maintain a wholly owned or majority-controlled legal entity registered in India, with a minimum 24x7 NOC and SOC staffed by a minimum of 50 India-based FTEs.
Vendor must hold active Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute Partner AND AWS Direct Connect Delivery Partner designations at the time of proposal submission.
Vendor must demonstrate a minimum of two (2) completed Managed SD-WAN deployments covering 100+ sites each in India, within the last four years, in the logistics, manufacturing, or BFSI sector.
Case Studies — Appendix C (format per Section 8.3)
Vendor, its subsidiaries, and key subcontractors must not be subject to any material economic sanction, debarrment from Government of India procurement, or CBI/ED enforcement action.

vlm_extraction.caption: 9.2 Weighted Scoring Model
rows:
• Requirements matrix compliance (TR-001 to TR-025) — 20%
• SD-WAN solution architecture quality & OEM credentials — 8%
• Cloud interconnect design & partner credentials — 6%
• Migration plan quality (phasing, ZTP, risk management) — 6%

• Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) over 36-month contract term — 18%
• OpEx hardware-as-a-service pricing model quality — 6%
• Commercial flexibility (payment milestones, volume discounts) — 6%

• ISO 27001, ISO 20000-1, SOC 2 Type II currency and scope — 8%
• SOC maturity: SIEM capability, analyst headcount, MTTR / MTTD evidence — 8%
• DPDP Act compliance framework & data localisation architecture — 4%

• Quality and relevance of case studies (Section 8.3) — 6%
• Verified client reference calls (shortlisted vendors only) — 4%
Minimum qualifying score to proceed to BAFO: 65 out of 100
